jokes.text: What we need to figure out is Greendale's obsession with group assignments. [Scoffs] Classic herd mentality.
jokes.setup: Jeff complains about group assignments being difficult
jokes.punchline: Britta turns it into pseudo-psychology about 'herd mentality'
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta - taking a mundane complaint and turning it into pretentious psychology. Good character integration.

jokes.text: All right, Britta doesn't have one. Who else?
jokes.setup: Britta offers her 'herd mentality' observation
jokes.punchline: Jeff dismisses it completely and moves on like she said nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - dismisses Britta's pseudo-intellectualism with brutal efficiency

jokes.text: I've been told I look like a Kennedy.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks for project ideas
jokes.punchline: Pierce offers completely irrelevant personal observation
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce non-sequitur, though not particularly clever

jokes.text: Nope, not an idea. That's a statement. Anyone not on medication?
jokes.setup: Pierce's Kennedy comment
jokes.punchline: Jeff's matter-of-fact correction escalates to questioning everyone's mental state
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from correction to medication joke, very Jeff

jokes.text: Professor Cornwaaa-- He didn't even finish signing his name.
jokes.setup: Reading assignment details
jokes.punchline: Professor's signature trails off mid-name, showing complete disengagement
jokes.explanation: Nice visual detail showing professor's complete apathy

jokes.text: Then, you might want to teach your mouth how to say 'we,' 'our,' and 'us.'
jokes.setup: Annie claiming to speak for both her and Shirley
jokes.punchline: Shirley's pointed grammar lesson about inclusive pronouns
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted Shirley comeback - polite but devastating. Great character voice and structure.

jokes.text: I've been told I look like a Kennedy. Nope, still just a random statement, and still useless.
jokes.setup: Pierce repeats the exact same line from before
jokes.punchline: Jeff's exasperated response acknowledges the repetition
jokes.explanation: Simple running gag - Pierce's obliviousness to Jeff's dismissal

jokes.text: Both: Of? Your first date? - Oh! - Da-doy.
jokes.setup: Annie asks about their one-year anniversary
jokes.punchline: Both Troy and Britta have no idea what anniversary she means
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing their relationship's lack of investment

jokes.text: That was a test, and you failed. - I was just kidding.
jokes.setup: Their obvious confusion about the anniversary
jokes.punchline: Both try to cover with unconvincing excuses
jokes.explanation: Transparent attempts to save face after being caught

jokes.text: Somebody's sure working her way around the table. Aren't you?
jokes.setup: Revelation that Troy and Britta are dating
jokes.punchline: Jeff implies Britta is sleeping with everyone in the group
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff snark - mean but in character. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Both: Oh... That, you remember?
jokes.setup: Abed remembers his and Troy's Freaky Friday anniversary perfectly
jokes.punchline: Troy and Britta are incredulous he remembers this while they forgot their own
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Abed remembers his movie anniversary while they forgot their relationship one

jokes.text: Judge Reinhold. Both: Oh.
jokes.setup: Abed listing body-switching movies
jokes.punchline: Everyone's disappointed reaction to Judge Reinhold reference
jokes.explanation: Subtle dig at Judge Reinhold's career with minimal setup

jokes.text: [Stilted] Oh, Troy, you are so thoughtful. I wish I had the capacity for sentimentality like you do.
jokes.setup: Troy's heartfelt gift presentation
jokes.punchline: Abed's robotic, stilted response to genuine emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - intellectualizing emotion instead of feeling it

jokes.text: [Stilted] Me? I wish I was more like you. No emotional hang-ups, endless cool adventures. You have it so easy. No, you do, Troy. No, you do, Abed.
jokes.setup: Both expressing envy of each other's traits
jokes.punchline: Escalates into childish 'No, you do' argument
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from sincere to childish, setting up body switch

jokes.text: Both: I wish I could switch places with you for just one day! [Babbling gibberish]
jokes.setup: Their escalating argument about wanting each other's lives
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous wish followed by incomprehensible babbling
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central conceit with appropriate absurdity

jokes.text: Sorry. Routine light switch check.
jokes.setup: Dramatic body-switching moment
jokes.punchline: Janitor interrupts with completely mundane, nonsensical task
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist interruption - 'routine light switch check' is beautifully nonsensical

jokes.text: I thought that would work. Yeah, we're all floored it didn't.
jokes.setup: Abed's disappointment that the body switch didn't work
jokes.punchline: Jeff's sarcastic response to their surprise
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff sarcasm - economical and in character

jokes.text: [Direct, as Abed] Yes. That would be this day.
jokes.setup: Britta wishing Troy happy anniversary
jokes.punchline: Troy responds in Abed's overly literal, robotic manner
jokes.explanation: Good Abed impersonation - overly literal response to romantic gesture

jokes.text: [Emoting, as Troy] Oh, my God. This is wrinkling my brain! And of all the days! I got my big, important meeting with my study group.
jokes.setup: Abed trying to act like Troy would
jokes.punchline: 'Wrinkling my brain' as Troy-esque malapropism, plus calling study group 'big important meeting'
jokes.explanation: 'Wrinkling my brain' is a great Troy-esque phrase, plus Abed's formal description of study group

jokes.text: Annie, remember. We're doing doable and passable. We're doing whatever it takes to help us... - Thank you. - ...beat Leonard.
jokes.setup: Jeff reminds Annie of their low-effort approach
jokes.punchline: He switches from lazy philosophy to competitive drive mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's hypocrisy - his laziness disappears when his ego is threatened

jokes.text: You're getting beat by Leonard? [Chuckles] He's so old.
jokes.setup: Leonard being valedictorian front-runner
jokes.punchline: Jeff's incredulous response focuses on Leonard's age
jokes.explanation: Simple age joke, not particularly clever but in character

jokes.text: [Low] Annie is the three. That is what 'respectively' suggests.
jokes.setup: Annie and Shirley are 'close three and two, respectively'
jokes.punchline: Annie's quiet correction about what 'respectively' means, showing she's third not second
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay joke - Annie's need to correct grammar even when it hurts her

jokes.text: Mm. Mm-hmm. [Chuckles] Whoa! That was weird. Mm-hmm. Mm-hmm. We didn't mean to do that. We know exactly where we sit.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed presumably sit in each other's usual spots
jokes.punchline: Awkward recognition they're in wrong places, over-explaining their mistake
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with awkward over-explanation typical of their dynamic

jokes.text: Both: Oh, thank God, you noticed.
jokes.setup: Someone asking what's going on with them
jokes.punchline: Their simultaneous relief at being caught
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat showing their desperation to be understood

jokes.text: Unfortunately, yes, dorks.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed asking if he remembers their body switch attempt
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dismissive 'unfortunately yes' plus calling them dorks
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff dismissal with economical insult

jokes.text: Both: I deserve to be number one! Sorry. Or me! Sorry.
jokes.setup: Discussion about getting an A on the project
jokes.punchline: Both Annie and Shirley simultaneously reveal their competitive nature, then awkwardly try to cover
jokes.explanation: Great reveal of both characters' hidden competitiveness with awkward recovery

jokes.text: Tell your boyfriend and his boyfriend that playtime is over.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed's body-switching antics
jokes.punchline: Jeff characterizes Troy as Britta's boyfriend and Abed as Troy's boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Clever way to reference Troy/Abed's intense friendship and dismiss their antics

jokes.text: Stay alive. Or don't. I'm on it.
jokes.setup: Jeff giving everyone assignments
jokes.punchline: Pierce's casual acceptance of potentially dying
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Pierce - taking death as casually as any other task. Great economy.

jokes.text: Oh, so he might need an almost therapist.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Troy going through issues
jokes.punchline: Britta describes herself as 'almost therapist' - not quite qualified
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta self-description - acknowledging her incomplete psychology training

jokes.text: Dean, I need you. [Gasps] It's happening. Shut the door.
jokes.setup: Jeff saying he needs the Dean
jokes.punchline: Dean's excited reaction suggests he thinks it's romantic/sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean misinterpretation - his crush on Jeff leads to wishful thinking

jokes.text: Oh, I love those movies! They should make more of them. They're a timeless tale.
jokes.setup: Mention of body-switching movies
jokes.punchline: Dean's enthusiastic but generic praise
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat - Dean's tendency to over-praise everything

jokes.text: Both: I wish I could switch places with you for just one day. Oh! Oh! Oh... Ah! [Gasps] Sorry. Routine light switch check. That is not a thing!
jokes.setup: Jeff and Dean accidentally recreating the body switch scenario
jokes.punchline: Same janitor interruption, but Jeff calls out that 'routine light switch check' makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalation - Jeff actually questions the nonsensical 'routine'

jokes.text: [Deep, as Jeff] Oh, you've got to be kidding me. I'm you?
jokes.setup: Body switch apparently happens between Jeff and Dean
jokes.punchline: Dean speaking in Jeff's deep voice with Jeff's characteristic exasperation
jokes.explanation: Good voice acting joke - Dean doing Jeff's signature exasperation

jokes.text: Of course. It's fricking Friday. There's no justification to be made, because it can't happen, weirdo.
jokes.setup: Apparent body switching happening
jokes.punchline: Jeff breaks character to point out the absurdity, mentioning it's Friday (episode day)
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment - Jeff acknowledging the show's Friday timeslot and absurd premises

jokes.text: You're not even holding a phone!
jokes.setup: Jeff apparently making phone calls
jokes.punchline: Dean points out Jeff's not actually holding a phone
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag pointing out Jeff's pretend phone conversation

jokes.text: He's like the pick-up sticks of people.
jokes.setup: Troy (as Abed) looking at his own body
jokes.punchline: Describes himself as 'pick-up sticks of people' - thin and gangly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor - comparing Troy's lanky frame to pick-up sticks game. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Well, well, well. John 'Mc-Lame.' Never thought I'd see your face again.
jokes.setup: Troy (as Abed) entering video store
jokes.punchline: 'John Mc-Lame' as insult pun on common name
jokes.explanation: Weak pun-based insult, not particularly clever

jokes.text: I would rather watch a napkin sit and do nothing all day than to even endure the trailer for Die Hard!
jokes.setup: Describing hatred of Die Hard
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific preference for watching a napkin over Die Hard trailer
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - napkin watching as preferable activity

jokes.text: I'm both starving and another word for wanting food.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining his hunger
jokes.punchline: Can't think of synonym for 'hungry,' admits he wants 'another word for wanting food'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy line - his brain short-circuiting trying to find a synonym. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Nice try. That's the remake. In the original, the mom and daughter simply exclaimed, 'I wish I could switch places with you for just one day,' like we did. In that one, there's a magic fortune cookie.
jokes.setup: Jeff presenting what he thinks is the right DVD
jokes.punchline: Troy (as Abed) correcting him with extensive film knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed behavior - obsessive film knowledge delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Listen, why don't I pitch you a remake of a remake, where my magic mouth tells you drop the act because Abed isn't even here. Then, you cough up the DVD and we go tell Abed you're back in your own bodies, so we can go do doable and passable banners.
jokes.setup: Troy insisting on the body switch reality
jokes.punchline: Jeff's elaborate 'pitch' for ending the charade using movie terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke - Jeff using movie pitch format to try to end their movie-based bit

jokes.text: First, that's a terrible movie. Second, I understand why you're frustrated, it's in your nature, but this is happening.
jokes.setup: Jeff's elaborate movie pitch
jokes.punchline: Troy dismisses it as 'terrible movie' then stays in character
jokes.explanation: Good commitment to bit - treating Jeff's pitch as actual movie pitch to critique

jokes.text: I'd stop what I'm doing, but then I'd be depriving you of the view. You're welcome. What's up?
jokes.setup: Annie and Shirley entering Dean's office
jokes.punchline: Dean thinks they want to look at his body
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean narcissism - assuming others want to admire his body

jokes.text: I'm in Greendale, stuck in the body of a man who could be Gollum's shadow, so yeah, I'd say it's half past suck.
jokes.setup: Asked if it's a bad time
jokes.punchline: 'Gollum's shadow' insult plus 'half past suck' as time expression
jokes.explanation: 'Gollum's shadow' is brilliantly specific insult for the Dean's appearance, 'half past suck' is creative time expression

jokes.text: [Laughs] Totally. 'Half past suck.' That's funny. Okay. That's creepy.
jokes.setup: Jeff's 'half past suck' line
jokes.punchline: Dean laughs at being insulted, then realizes it's creepy behavior
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Dean's inappropriate enjoyment of Jeff's attention even when insulting

jokes.text: Back in 1968, Leonard got an 'A' in Rotary Phone Maintenance.
jokes.setup: Investigating Leonard's perfect GPA
jokes.punchline: Absurdly outdated class from 1968
jokes.explanation: 'Rotary Phone Maintenance' is perfect absurd vintage class name that explains Leonard's age advantage

jokes.text: It means we're taking an old man down. [Gasps] Ooh.
jokes.setup: Learning Leonard's GPA strategy
jokes.punchline: Annie's dark declaration followed by Shirley's shocked gasp
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Annie's ruthless competitiveness vs Shirley's shock at targeting elderly

jokes.text: What is wrong with you? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Dean's behavior being questioned
jokes.punchline: Jeff's honest admission of not understanding Dean
jokes.explanation: Simple but honest character moment - Jeff's confusion at Dean's behavior

jokes.text: The Greenpeace one you haven't gotten yet because you're afraid of needles.
jokes.setup: Troy knowing about Britta's tattoo
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's a planned Greenpeace tattoo she's too scared to get
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta detail - wannabe activist too scared of needles for commitment tattoo

jokes.text: [Laughter] Yes, Lord Brickell, I was indeed down by the stables.
jokes.setup: Troy and Jeff entering janitor area
jokes.punchline: Janitor role-playing elaborate period drama scenario
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd - janitors doing elaborate murder mystery roleplay during work

jokes.text: There you go. My work here is done. You're one and two again. Now, you can direct your anger and resentment at each other. Congrats. - Classic wrap-up. - Shut up, Leonard! I've got a picture of your old nose! - It was a lateral move!
jokes.setup: Leonard announcing he's solved their ranking problem
jokes.punchline: Annie's specific threat about nose job photos, Leonard's defensive response
jokes.explanation: 'I've got a picture of your old nose' is brilliantly specific blackmail threat, Leonard's 'lateral move' defense is perfect

jokes.text: Holy--makes complete sense at this school! FYI, one of these piles is load-bearing, so, uh, yeah, watch that.
jokes.setup: Seeing the chaotic lost and found
jokes.punchline: Jeff's resignation plus Dean's warning about load-bearing junk pile
jokes.explanation: 'Load-bearing' junk pile is absurdly perfect for Greendale's incompetence

jokes.text: I wish I had the courage to commit to a bit like you, Abed, or to a relationship, like Troy. Committing is hard, because we're all scared of what happens when we find out we committed to the wrong thing, but there's no shame in what Troy's done. He put himself out there and he really tried, and that proves he cares.
jokes.setup: Jeff reflecting on Troy and Abed's commitment
jokes.punchline: Sincere moment about courage to commit, even when things don't work out
jokes.explanation: Rare sincere Jeff moment with genuine wisdom about commitment and courage

jokes.text: Both: I wish I had my own body back! [Babbling gibberish]
jokes.setup: Troy deciding to end the charade
jokes.punchline: Reversal of original body switch with same gibberish
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback that reverses the original switch scenario

jokes.text: Sorry, routine light switch check. [Babbling continues]
jokes.setup: Body switch reversal happening
jokes.punchline: Same janitor with same nonsensical interruption
jokes.explanation: Consistent running gag with the absurd 'routine light switch check'

jokes.text: Holy--makes no sense whatsoever. Whole thing took 25 minutes tops.
jokes.setup: Pierce claiming he did the whole project
jokes.punchline: Annie's shock at his apparently perfect banners, Pierce's casual time estimate
jokes.explanation: Good payoff - Pierce unexpectedly being competent at the thing Annie stressed about

jokes.text: When I switched bodies with Jeffrey... - Nope. - I thought I knew what it would be like to have Jeffrey inside of me... - That did not happen. - But as it turns out, having Jeffrey inside of me... - Nope again. - ...only brought out the worst in me. Which is to say, having Jeffrey inside of me... - No one was inside of anyone! - ...was wrong... - to have Jeffrey inside of me. - Shut up!
jokes.setup: Dean trying to apologize for his behavior
jokes.punchline: Repeated sexual innuendo with 'Jeffrey inside of me' while Jeff protests
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Dean's obliviousness to sexual innuendo while Jeff grows increasingly frustrated

jokes.text: Oh, man. Football is fun. That is an interest I have. Also plumbing and air conditioning are things I think about.
jokes.setup: Troy trying to act normal/straight
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly overcompensating with stereotypical masculine interests
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Troy's awkward overcompensation after emotional vulnerability
